description: University-integrated platform for student portfolio generation, project verification, and talent discovery

sprints.title: Sprint 1 — Backend Foundation Setup (NestJS Modular Architecture + Health Endpoint)
sprints.sprintGoal: Set up a production-ready NestJS backend with a clean modular architecture, configuration, and a working /health endpoint so frontend + backend integration can start immediately.

willDeliver: Health module with GET /health returning service status
Swagger (optional but recommended) for quick API visibility

deliverables: /health endpoint reachable locally and returns JSON

userStories.story: As a developer, I can run the backend locally and confirm it's alive via /health.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install completes successfully
npm run start:dev runs without errors
GET /health responds with 200 status
Response contains {status: 'ok', service: 'api', timestamp}

userStories.story: As a developer, I can add new modules (users, auth, etc.) using a consistent pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Modules folder structure exists and is documented
Example module demonstrates the pattern
README includes module generation guide

userStories.story: As a developer, I can manage config via env variables without hardcoding secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: .env file is git-ignored
.env.example exists with all required keys
ConfigService provides typed access to config
All secrets are retrieved from environment

tasks.title: Initialize NestJS project with proper structure
tasks.description: Create new NestJS app, set up folder structure for modular architecture
subtasks: Create src/common directory (filters, pipes, interceptors, utils)

tasks.title: Setup environment configuration with @nestjs/config
tasks.description: Configure environment variables, add .env and .env.example files
subtasks: Create ConfigModule and import in AppModule
Add NODE_ENV, PORT, DATABASE_URL, GITHUB_OAUTH_ID, GITHUB_OAUTH_SECRET keys

tasks.title: Add global validation pipeline
tasks.description: Setup ValidationPipe globally for request validation
subtasks: Add ValidationPipe to AppModule providers or main.ts
Configure with whitelist: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true, transform: true
Type validation will be done per endpoint using DTOs
Test with a simple POST request with invalid data

tasks.title: Create HealthModule with /health endpoint
tasks.description: Implement health check endpoint for service verification
subtasks: Response: { status: 'ok', service: 'api', timestamp: ISO timestamp, version: '0.1.0' }
Test endpoint manually with curl or Postman

tasks.title: Setup Swagger/OpenAPI documentation
tasks.description: Add Swagger for API documentation and testing
subtasks: Add basic info: CandidStudent API, version 0.1.0, description

tasks.title: Add CORS configuration
tasks.description: Enable CORS for frontend development and integration
subtasks: Configure allowed origins from env variable
Test CORS headers with OPTIONS request

tasks.title: Setup npm scripts for development
tasks.description: Add useful scripts for development workflow

tasks.title: Create Docker setup for local development
tasks.description: Setup Docker and docker-compose for isolated environment
subtasks: Create Dockerfile for NestJS app (dev stage)

tasks.title: Update README with setup and run instructions
tasks.description: Document how to setup, run, and develop the backend
subtasks: Add Installation steps (git clone, npm install)

acceptanceCriteria: npm install completes without errors
npm run start:dev runs successfully and app listens on configured PORT
GET /health responds with 200 status and JSON body
Response body includes: status, service, timestamp
Code is organized in modules (clearly visible folder structure)
All config comes from .env (no hardcoded values in code)
Global ValidationPipe is active
Swagger docs available at /api/docs
README includes all setup and usage instructions
Docker image builds and container runs without errors

sprints.title: Sprint 2 — Database Layer Setup (PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM + Migrations)
sprints.sprintGoal: Establish a production-grade database layer with Drizzle ORM, schema versioning via migrations, and documented data models ready for feature development.

willDeliver: Drizzle ORM setup with TypeScript types

deliverables: README updated with database setup instructions
TypeScript schema types generated from Drizzle
Database diagram or entity relationship document

userStories.story: As a developer, I can create, run, and rollback database migrations without losing data.
acceptanceCriteria: drizzle migrate command works
New migration files are created with timestamps
Migrations can be run and rolled back safely
Schema changes are tracked in migrations folder
Previous migrations remain intact

userStories.story: As a backend developer, I can query the database using type-safe Drizzle ORM syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Drizzle client is injectable in services
Type hints work for all table queries
Relations between tables are properly typed
Query examples in a sample service

userStories.story: As a QA engineer, I can seed the database with test data for local testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed script exists and runs without errors
Script populates users, universities, teachers, students
Script can be run multiple times safely (idempotent)
README includes seed command

tasks.title: Install and configure Drizzle ORM
tasks.description: Setup Drizzle ORM as the database access layer
subtasks: Add migration commands to package.json scripts

tasks.title: Setup PostgreSQL connection with pooling
tasks.description: Configure database connection with connection pooling for performance
subtasks: Use postgres client with pool configuration
Add connection error handling and logging

tasks.title: Define initial schema with Drizzle
tasks.description: Create schema file with core entities
subtasks: Define users table (id, email, password_hash, name, role, created_at, updated_at)
Define universities table (id, name, location, admin_id, created_at, updated_at)
Define university_admins table (id, name, email, university_id, password_hash, is_active)
Define teachers table (id, name, email, university_id, password_hash, is_verified, is_active)
Define students table (id, name, email, university_id, password_hash, is_verified, is_active)
Define employers table (id, name, email, company, password_hash, is_active)
Add foreign key relationships between tables
Add timestamps to all tables with defaults

tasks.title: Create and run initial migration
tasks.description: Generate and execute the first database migration
subtasks: Review generated migration SQL for accuracy
Run 'drizzle-kit migrate' to apply migration to database
Verify tables exist in PostgreSQL with correct schema
Test rollback capability with test data

tasks.title: Create DatabaseModule for NestJS dependency injection
tasks.description: Setup database service and module for app-wide access
subtasks: Export all schema types from database module for use in services

tasks.title: Create database seed script
tasks.description: Script to populate local database with sample data
subtasks: Insert 1 sample admin user for each university
Insert 3-5 sample teachers for each university
Make script idempotent (check before insert or use upsert)
Add 'npm run seed' command to package.json
Document seed script usage in README

tasks.title: Update README with database setup instructions
tasks.description: Document database setup and migration process
subtasks: Add steps: npm run migrate, npm run seed
Add schema documentation or entity diagram
Document how to reset database for development

acceptanceCriteria: PostgreSQL is running and accessible via DATABASE_URL
Drizzle ORM is installed and configured correctly
drizzle.config.ts exists and is valid
Initial migration runs without errors
All tables exist in database with correct schema and indexes
DatabaseModule is injectable in NestJS services
npm run seed populates test data successfully
Connection pooling is configured and working
All queries are type-safe with Drizzle types
Migration files are tracked in git (src/database/migrations/)
Rollback of migrations works without data loss
outOfScope: Advanced query optimization (will be done based on usage patterns)
Query performance monitoring and caching (covered in later sprints)

sprints.title: Sprint 3 — Authentication Module (Registration, Login, JWT, RBAC Guards)
sprints.sprintGoal: Implement complete authentication system with registration, login, JWT tokens, and role-based access control (RBAC) guards so protected endpoints can be built in subsequent sprints.

willDeliver: User registration endpoint (email, password, role selection)
User login endpoint with JWT token issuance
JWT token validation and refresh mechanism
GET /auth/me endpoint for current user context

deliverables: AuthModule with registration, login, and token endpoints
Updated README with auth flow documentation
Example: protected /me endpoint returning user profile

userStories.story: As a user, I can register with email, password, and role (student/teacher/admin/employer).
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/register accepts { email, password, name, role }
Password is hashed with Argon2 before storage
Response is { id, email, name, role, createdAt } (no password)
Duplicate emails are rejected with 409 Conflict
Invalid email format is rejected with 400
Weak passwords are rejected
All required fields are validated

userStories.story: As a user, I can login with email and password and receive a JWT token.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/login accepts { email, password }
Response is { accessToken, expiresIn, user: {...} }
Invalid credentials return 401 Unauthorized
Token can be used in Authorization header for authenticated requests
Token expires after configured duration
Expired tokens return 401

userStories.story: As a developer, I can protect routes by role using @Roles(Role.STUDENT) decorator.
acceptanceCriteria: @Roles decorator exists and is easy to use
Requests without proper role get 403 Forbidden
Multiple roles can be specified: @Roles(Role.STUDENT, Role.TEACHER)
Role checking is enforced at request level
Unauthenticated requests get 401 before role check

userStories.story: As an authenticated user, I can fetch my profile via GET /auth/me.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /auth/me returns current user profile
Unauthenticated requests return 401
Response includes id, email, name, role, createdAt
Token must be valid (not expired)

tasks.title: Install authentication dependencies
tasks.description: Add required packages for JWT and password hashing
subtasks: Verify versions align with NestJS and Node version

tasks.title: Create Auth DTOs with Zod validation
tasks.description: Define request/response schemas for auth endpoints
subtasks: Define Zod schema: email, password, name, role
Add password strength validation (min 8 chars, uppercase, number, symbol)
Create auth response DTOs (token response, user response)

tasks.title: Create AuthService with password hashing and JWT logic
tasks.description: Core authentication business logic
subtasks: Implement register() method with duplicate email check
Implement login() method with credential validation
Implement validatePassword() to verify hashed password
Handle email uniqueness validation against database
Error handling: duplicate email (409), invalid credentials (401)
Return user object without password field
Add timeout for password hashing operations

tasks.title: Create AuthController with register and login endpoints
tasks.description: HTTP endpoints for authentication
subtasks: Add @Public() decorator to register and login endpoints
Add error handling with proper HTTP status codes
Add Swagger decorators for API documentation

tasks.title: Setup JWT strategy for token validation
tasks.description: Configure Passport JWT strategy
subtasks: Verify token with JWT_SECRET from config
Return user payload on successful verification
Test with valid and expired tokens

tasks.title: Create RolesGuard for RBAC enforcement
tasks.description: Guard to check user roles on protected endpoints
subtasks: Get required roles from @Roles() decorator metadata
Check if user role matches one of the required roles
Return 403 Forbidden if role doesn't match
Allow requests with @Public() decorator to bypass role check
Handle unauthenticated requests (401 before role check)
Test with multiple roles and single role

tasks.title: Create @Roles(), @Public(), and @CurrentUser() decorators
tasks.description: Easy-to-use decorators for route protection and context
subtasks: Add JSDoc comments for each decorator

tasks.title: Setup JwtModule and RolesGuard in AuthModule
tasks.description: Configure JWT and role-based access in NestJS
subtasks: Use JwtModule.register() with secret and expiration options
Read JWT_SECRET and JWT_EXPIRATION from ConfigService
Configure RolesGuard as APP_GUARD for global enforcement

tasks.title: Add comprehensive test coverage for auth module
tasks.description: Unit tests for auth service and controllers
subtasks: Test registration with invalid email format
Test password hashing (verify hash can't be reversed)
Test @Roles guard with valid and invalid roles

tasks.title: Update README with authentication guide
tasks.description: Document auth flow and usage
subtasks: Document flow: register → login → use token
Add security notes (HTTPS in production, token rotation)

acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/register works with valid data and returns user without password
POST /auth/login returns JWT access token on success
GET /auth/me returns 401 Unauthorized without token
GET /auth/me returns authenticated user profile with valid token
Routes with @Roles(Role.STUDENT) reject non-student users with 403
Passwords are hashed with Argon2 (never stored or returned in plain text)
Duplicate emails are rejected with 409 Conflict
JWT tokens expire after configured duration (e.g., 1 hour)
RolesGuard is applied globally to all controllers
All auth endpoints are documented in Swagger (/api/docs)
Unit tests have >80% coverage for auth module
Auth module can be used immediately for protecting other endpoints
outOfScope: Rate limiting on auth endpoints (covered in later sprint)
Token refresh/rotation (basic JWT only for MVP)

summary.estimatedDuration: 26 weeks (6 months) for MVP + extended features
summary.mvcScope: Sprints 1-14 (14 weeks) → auth, user mgmt, projects, verification, portfolio, search
summary.extendedScope: Sprints 15-25 → GitHub, employer features, AI, analytics, deployment, security, launch
notes: All story points are estimates; adjust based on team capacity and experience
Sprints can be run in parallel (e.g., frontend Sprint 10 while backend Sprint 6)
Backend Sprints 1-9 form the foundation; frontend can start on Sprint 10
Sprints 19-25 are supporting tasks (testing, DevOps, security, launch)
AI features (Sprint 22) are optional; can be added post-launch for revenue
Payment integration (Stripe) in Sprint 24 is required for revenue generation
Each sprint includes detailed user stories, tasks, and acceptance criteria
Definition of Done: code review, tests passing, documented, merged to main
